notes: Cream Finance Oct-27-2021. Attacker flash-borrowed ~$1.5B of DAI/ETH, minted yUSD (yvDAI vault shares), then donated DAI directly into the underlying Curve y-pool to spike yvDAI.pricePerShare. Cream priced yUSD collateral off that inflated per-share value and let the attacker borrow out every remaining asset (USDC, USDT, ETH, WBTC, etc.). Either price_oracle_manipulation OR donation_attack is a valid classification — the donation is the MEANS, the inflated oracle reading is the EXPLOITED PRIMITIVE.

causal_factors.description: Borrows ~500M DAI from MakerDAO + additional capital from Aave/dYdX to fund the donation
Deposits DAI into Yearn yvDAI vault to obtain yUSD shares used as Cream collateral
Transfers DAI directly into the Curve y-pool (outside the deposit flow) so yvDAI.pricePerShare doubles
Borrows USDC/USDT/ETH/WBTC from Cream against the now-2x-valued yUSD position, draining the markets

blast_radius: Affected protocols: at minimum the Cream Finance Comptroller (lender of last resort drained); cascading risk on Yearn yvDAI and Curve y-pool depositors
Cascading risks: $130M USD of borrowed tokens left Cream — USDC, USDT, DAI, ETH, WBTC market liquidity dropped to ~0
Recommendations: oracle should not use single-source pricePerShare; protect against direct token donations via TWAP or burn-tracked share accounting
what_to_look_for.diagram_md: docs/diagram_cream_<timestamp>.md should show: 1 flash_loan_borrow, multiple token_transfer actions with the Curve y-pool address as recipient (the donation), and follow-on token_transfers FROM Cream contracts TO the attacker contract
